product_description: Houghton Mifflin Harcourt (HMH) is a K-12 learning company that provides integrated curriculum, assessment, and professional learning solutions to educators globally. They deliver digital-first EdTech products designed to foster growth and support diverse learning needs across various subjects like literacy, math, science, and social studies.

ai_insights: HMH demonstrates a strong commitment to accessibility, integrating Universal Design for Learning principles into its curriculum materials and learning tools. Content development practices align with Web Content Accessibility Guidelines (WCAG) and actively work to improve standardized practices. HMH Ed, a core platform, supports features like screen reader functionality, keyboard navigation, accessible video, alternative text, and magnification tools for both students and teachers. An Accessibility Advisory Board helps drive consistency across products. While detailed accessibility features and policy statements are provided, a comprehensive, publicly available Accessibility Conformance Report (ACR) or Voluntary Product Accessibility Template (VPAT) document was not explicitly found. Third-party insights from educational institutions and standards bodies frequently mention HMH products in the context of supporting diverse learners, affirming efforts in language development and reading intervention programs, and highlighting engagement with web accessibility standards.
